```
Orthotopic Liver Transplantation for Urea Cycle Enzyme Deficiency

SATORU TODO,1, 2 THOMAS E. STARZL,1, 2 ANDREAS TZAKIS,1, 2 KEITH J. BENKOV,3 FRANTISEK KALOUSEK,4 TAKEyORI SAHEKI,5 KYuiCHI TanikAWA,6 AND WayNE A. FEnTOn4

1Department of Surgery, University Health Center of Pittsburgh, University of Pittsburgh, and 2the Veterans Administration Medical Center, Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania; 3Department of Pediatrics, Mount Sinai Medical Center, New York, New York 10029-6574; 4Department of Human Genetics, Yale University, New Haven, Connecticut; 5Department of Biochemistry, Kagoshima University, Kagoshima, Japan; and 6Department of Medicine, Kurume University, Kurume, Japan.

Hyperammonemia, abnormalities in plasma amino acids and abnormalities of standard liver functions were corrected by orthotopic liver transplantation in a 14-day-old boy with carbamyl phosphate synthetase-I deficiency and in a 35-yr-old man with argininosuccinic acid synthetase deficiency. The first patient had high plasma glutamine levels and no measureable citrulline, whereas citrulline values were markedly increased in Patient 2. Enzyme analysis of the original livers showed undetectable activity of carbamyl phosphate synthetase-I in Patient 1 and arginosuccinic acid synthetase in Patient 2. Both patients were comatose before surgery. Intellectual recovery of patient 1 has been slightly retarded because of a brain abscess caused by Aspergillus infection after surgery. Both patients are well at 34 and 40 mo, respectively, after surgery. Our experience has shown that orthotopic liver transplantation corrects the life-threatening metabolic abnormalities caused by deficiencies in the urea cycle enzymes carbamyl phosphate synthetase-I and arginosuccinic acid synthetase. Seven other patients-six with ornithine transcarbamylase deficiency and another with carbamyl phosphate synthetase-I deficiency-are known to have been treated elsewhere with liver transplantation 1/2 yr or longer ago. Four of these seven recipients also are well, with follow-ups of 1½/2 to 5 yr. Thus liver transplantation corrects the metabolic abnormalities of three of the six urea cycle enzyme deficiencies, and presumably would correct all. (HEPATOLOGY 1992;15:419-422.)

It was proposed in 1975 by Dr. Leon Rosenberg of Yale University that liver transplantation would allow somatic correction of diseases caused by deficiency of any of the six urea cycle enzymes (Personal communication,

1975). The liver enzymes responsible for biosynthesis of urea from its principal precursors (ammonium and glutamine) are N-acetylglutamate synthetase, carbamyl phosphate synthetase-I (CPS-I), ornithine transcarbamylase (OTC), argininosuccinic acid synthetase (ASS), argininosuccinase and arginase (1). N-acetylglutamate synthetase deficiency has been reported in one man (2). OTC deficiency is an X chromosome-linked disorder, and the other 4 deficiencies are autosomal recessive traits. High plasma ammonium and glutamine levels are thought to cause astrocyte swelling, brain edema and secondary neuron injury (3), with variable neurological or behavioral consequences that may be acute or chronic. We report here the amelioration of such complications after liver transplantation in a 14-day-old white boy with CPS-I deficiency and a 35-yr-old Japanese man with ASS deficiency.

CASE REPORTS

Case 1.An apparently normal, full-term male child of first-cousin parents became bradycardic and apneic shortly after birth on May 18, 1988, requiring endotracheal intubation. By postpartum day 2, he was unresponsive to pain stimuli, although results of a computed tomographic scan of the head were normal. Serum ammonia level on day 6 was 387 μmol/L, total bilirubin was 202 μmol/L (direct bilirubin = 27.4 μmol/L) and serum transaminases were normal. Peritoneal dialysis on day 7 reduced serum ammonia level from 514 to 160 μmol/L. With the diagnosis of CPS-I urea cycle enzyme deficiency, the patient was treated with an intravenous infusion of arginine, sodium benzoate and sodium phenylacetate supplemented with Intralipid and 5% dextrose (4), and tube feeding with polyxose and protein-free Mead Johnson formula 80056 (Mead Johnson, Evansville, IN). After transfer to the University of Pittsburgh, orthotopic liver transplantation (OLT) was performed with a newborn cadaver liver on June 2, 1988, at 14 days of age. Biliary drainage was performed by choledochojejunostomy (Roux-en-Y anastomosis). The surgery and subsequent immunosuppression with cyclosporine and prednisone were performed with standard techniques (5).

Severe rejection beginning in postoperative wk 3 necessitated augmented steroid dosage and the addition of OKT3. An Aspergillus brain abscess was treated successfully with antibiotics, open drainage and, ultimately, ventriculoperitoneal shunt. Bacteremia and systemic cytomegalovirus infection further complicated the course, necessitating hospitalization for 71/z mo (6). Subsequently, the child lived at home without special domiciliary care. Growth and development have been uninterrupted during the 40 mo of follow-up, with a weight gain from 2.
```

augmented steroid dosage and the addition of OKT3. An Aspergillus brain abscess was treated successfully with antibiotics, open drainage and, ultimately, ventriculoperitoneal shunt. Growth and development have been uninterrupted during the 40 mo of follow-up, with a weight gain from 2.5 to 20 kg on an unrestricted diet. Liver function is normal, and most the recent blood ammonia level after 37 mo was 34 μmol/L (normal< 39 μmol/L).

Case 2. A 35-yr-old Japanese man was the second son of healthy first-cousin parents. He had a normal brother and sister and was the father of a healthy daughter. He was ostensibly normal until three episodes of temporary altered consciousness and behavioral aberrations occurred in January, April and May 1987. After initial psychiatric hospitalization, he was transferred to Kurume University, where his serum ammonium and serum citrulline levels were 264 and 445 μmol/L, respectively (normal, <39 and <52 μmol/L). On diagnosis of ASS deficiency, he was prescribed sodium benzoate, arginine and branched amino acid infusion, with restriction of oral protein intake. During the following 18 mo, he had four major episodes of serum ammonium elevation, with transient bouts of stage IV coma, which were successfully treated by hemodialysis. Because of deterioration and stage III and IV coma necessitating ventilator support, he was flown in November 1988 to the University of Pittsburgh, where OLT was performed on December 23, 1987, with biliary drainage by duct-to-duct anastomosis.

Preoperative total serum bilirubin of 425 μmol/L fell to normal within a few days. A computed tomographic scan of the head in November 1988 had revealed cortical atrophy. Postoperatively, complete recovery of consciousness and removal from ventilator support required several weeks, but convalescence was otherwise uncomplicated under cyclosporine-steroid therapy (5). The patient was discharged from the hospital after 44 days and has been at home since then. Recovery of neuromotor and intellectual capabilities required many months, but eventually the patient returned to his previous position as vice president of a construction company. Liver function is normal 34 mo postoperatively. Blood ammonia levels have been 7 to 21 μmol/L (normal < 39 μmol/L).

BIOCHEMICAL METHODS

Amino Acids. Plasma amino acids in patient 1 were measured 9 days after birth (5 days before transplantation) by an automated amino acid analyzer. At 34 mo after OLT, amino acids were determined by column chromatography (7). Amino acid separation was performed by a method modified from Hamilton (8). In Patient 2, the plasma amino acids were measured in the absence of treatment, 5 to 7 mo before transplantation, with a automated amino acid analyzer. The examinations were repeated 3, 4, and 20 mo after OLT.

Liver Enzymes. The removed native livers were immediately cut into small pieces, frozen with liquid nitrogen and stored at  80° C until the analyses of urea cycle enzymes. CPS-I and OTC were measured in Patient 1 in the Department of Human Genetics of the Yale University School of Medicine. Determinations were with a small-scale adaptation of the method of

Nuzum and Snodgrass (9). The liver tissues from Patient 2 were analyzed for CPS-I, OTC, ASS and AS activity in the Department of Biochemistry of Kagoshima University, Kagoshima, Japan, using a previously described method (10).

RESULTS

Plasma Amino Acids. The pretransplant plasma in Patient 1 had elevated levels of glutamine. Citrulline was undetectable, and the other measured amino acids were relatively normal (Table 1). Plasma amino acids 34 mo after surgery were normal. Citrulline levels became detectable.

The plasma amino acids in Patient 2 before and after transplantation are summarized in Table 2. The extremely high preoperative levels of citrulline and the modest elevations of arginine were corrected at all measured postoperative times. However, ornithine level (normal = 40 to 100 μmol/L) was slightly elevated at 3 mo (153 µmol/L) and at 20 mo (124 μmol/L) postoperatively.

Tissue Studies. On light microscopy, the native liver of patient 1 had only minor, nonspecific abnormalities. Similarly, electron microscopic studies were normal, except for nonspecific elongation of the mitochondria. CPS-I activity was undetectable in the homogenate of this liver. OTC activity was normal when compared with that of a control liver sample (Table 3).

The native liver of patient 2 was enlarged, with cholestasis and massive steatosis and hemosiderosis. Periportal fibrosis, but no cirrhosis, was present. The liver homogenate was devoid of ASS activity, but exhibited significant CPS-1, OTC, and AS activity (Table 4).

DISCUSSION

The diagnosis of urea cycle enzyme deficiency and the specific enzyme involved were established with certainty in our two cases. Both had hyperammonemia and plasma amino acid abnormalities, which permitted the presumptive diagnosis of the enzyme deficiency. The diagnoses were confirmed by analysis of the subsequently excised livers. To our knowledge, the 2-wk-old infant with CPS-I deficiency is the youngest patient ever
```

---Task---
Based on the last extraction task, identify and extract any **missed or incorrectly formatted** entities and relationships from the input text.

---Instructions---
1.  **Strict Adherence to System Format:** Strictly adhere to all format requirements for entity and relationship lists, including output order, field delimiters, and proper noun handling, as specified in the system instructions.
2.  **Focus on Corrections/Additions:**
    *   **Do NOT** re-output entities and relationships that were **correctly and fully** extracted in the last task.
    *   If an entity or relationship was **missed** in the last task, extract and output it now according to the system format.
    *   If an entity or relationship was **truncated, had missing fields, or was otherwise incorrectly formatted** in the last task, re-output the *corrected and complete* version in the specified format.
3.  **Output Format - Entities:** Output a total of 4 fields for each entity, delimited by `<|#|>`, on a single line. The first field *must* be the literal string `entity`.
4.  **Output Format - Relationships:** Output a total of 5 fields for each relationship, delimited by `<|#|>`, on a single line. The first field *must* be the literal string `relation`.
5.  **Output Content Only:** Output *only* the extracted list of entities and relationships. Do not include any introductory or concluding remarks, explanations, or additional text before or after the list.
6.  **Completion Signal:** Output `<|COMPLETE|>` as the final line after all relevant missing or corrected entities and relationships have been extracted and presented.
7.  **Output Language:** Ensure the output language is English. Proper nouns (e.g., personal names, place names, organization names) must be kept in their original language and not translated.

<Output>
---Role---
You are a Knowledge Graph Specialist responsible for extracting entities and relationships from the input text.

---Instructions---
1.  **Entity Extraction & Output:**
    *   **Identification:** Identify clearly defined and meaningful entities in the input text.
    *   **Entity Details:** For each identified entity, extract the following information:
        *   `entity_name`: The name of the entity. If the entity name is case-insensitive, capitalize the first letter of each significant word (title case). Ensure **consistent naming** across the entire extraction process.
        *   `entity_type`: Categorize the entity using one of the following types: `Person,Creature,Organization,Location,Event,Concept,Method,Content,Data,Artifact,NaturalObject`. If none of the provided entity types apply, do not add new entity type and classify it as `Other`.
        *   `entity_description`: Provide a concise yet comprehensive description of the entity's attributes and activities, based *solely* on the information present in the input text.
    *   **Output Format - Entities:** Output a total of 4 fields for each entity, delimited by `<|#|>`, on a single line. The first field *must* be the literal string `entity`.
        *   Format: `entity<|#|>entity_name<|#|>entity_type<|#|>entity_description`

2.  **Relationship Extraction & Output:**
    *   **Identification:** Identify direct, clearly stated, and meaningful relationships between previously extracted entities.
    *   **N-ary Relationship Decomposition:** If a single statement describes a relationship involving more than two entities (an N-ary relationship), decompose it into multiple binary (two-entity) relationship pairs for separate description.
        *   **Example:** For "Alice, Bob, and Carol collaborated on Project X," extract binary relationships such as "Alice collaborated with Project X," "Bob collaborated with Project X," and "Carol collaborated with Project X," or "Alice collaborated with Bob," based on the most reasonable binary interpretations.
    *   **Relationship Details:** For each binary relationship, extract the following fields:
        *   `source_entity`: The name of the source entity. Ensure **consistent naming** with entity extraction. Capitalize the first letter of each significant word (title case) if the name is case-insensitive.
        *   `target_entity`: The name of the target entity. Ensure **consistent naming** with entity extraction. Capitalize the first letter of each significant word (title case) if the name is case-insensitive.
        *   `relationship_keywords`: One or more high-level keywords summarizing the overarching nature, concepts, or themes of the relationship. Multiple keywords within this field must be separated by a comma `,`. **DO NOT use `<|#|>` for separating multiple keywords within this field.**
        *   `relationship_description`: A concise explanation of the nature of the relationship between the source and target entities, providing a clear rationale for their connection.
    *   **Output Format - Relationships:** Output a total of 5 fields for each relationship, delimited by `<|#|>`, on a single line. The first field *must* be the literal string `relation`.
        *   Format: `relation<|#|>source_entity<|#|>target_entity<|#|>relationship_keywords<|#|>relationship_description`

3.  **Delimiter Usage Protocol:**
    *   The `<|#|>` is a complete, atomic marker and **must not be filled with content**. It serves strictly as a field separator.
    *   **Incorrect Example:** `entity<|#|>Tokyo<|location|>Tokyo is the capital of Japan.`
    *   **Correct Example:** `entity<|#|>Tokyo<|#|>location<|#|>Tokyo is the capital of Japan.`

4.  **Relationship Direction & Duplication:**
    *   Treat all relationships as **undirected** unless explicitly stated otherwise. Swapping the source and target entities for an undirected relationship does not constitute a new relationship.
    *   Avoid outputting duplicate relationships.

5.  **Output Order & Prioritization:**
    *   Output all extracted entities first, followed by all extracted relationships.
    *   Within the list of relationships, prioritize and output those relationships that are **most significant** to the core meaning of the input text first.

6.  **Context & Objectivity:**
    *   Ensure all entity names and descriptions are written in the **third person**.
    *   Explicitly name the subject or object; **avoid using pronouns** such as `this article`, `this paper`, `our company`, `I`, `you`, and `he/she`.

7.  **Language & Proper Nouns:**
    *   The entire output (entity names, keywords, and descriptions) must be written in `English`.
    *   Proper nouns (e.g., personal names, place names, organization names) should be retained in their original language if a proper, widely accepted translation is not available or would cause ambiguity.

8.  **Completion Signal:** Output the literal string `<|COMPLETE|>` only after all entities and relationships, following all criteria, have been completely extracted and outputted.

---Examples---
<Entity_types>
["Person","Creature","Organization","Location","Event","Concept","Method","Content","Data","Artifact","NaturalObject"]

Then Taylor did something unexpected. They paused beside Jordan and, for a moment, observed the device with something akin to reverence. "If this tech can be understood..." Taylor said, their voice quieter, "It could change the game for us. For all of us."

The underlying dismissal earlier seemed to falter, replaced by a glimpse of reluctant respect for the gravity of what lay in their hands. Jordan looked up, and for a fleeting heartbeat, their eyes locked with Taylor's, a wordless clash of wills softening into an uneasy truce.

---Instructions---
1.  **Strict Adherence to System Format:** Strictly adhere to all format requirements for entity and relationship lists, including output order, field delimiters, and proper noun handling, as specified in the system instructions.
2.  **Focus on Corrections/Additions:**
    *   **Do NOT** re-output entities and relationships that were **correctly and fully** extracted in the last task.
    *   If an entity or relationship was **missed** in the last task, extract and output it now according to the system format.
    *   If an entity or relationship was **truncated, had missing fields, or was otherwise incorrectly formatted** in the last task, re-output the *corrected and complete* version in the specified format.
3.  **Output Format - Entities:** Output a total of 4 fields for each entity, delimited by `<|#|>`, on a single line. The first field *must* be the literal string `entity`.
4.  **Output Format - Relationships:** Output a total of 5 fields for each relationship, delimited by `<|#|>`, on a single line. The first field *must* be the literal string `relation`.
5.  **Output Content Only:** Output *only* the extracted list of entities and relationships. Do not include any introductory or concluding remarks, explanations, or additional text before or after the list.
6.  **Completion Signal:** Output `<|COMPLETE|>` as the final line after all relevant missing or corrected entities and relationships have been extracted and presented.
7.  **Output Language:** Ensure the output language is English. Proper nouns (e.g., personal names, place names, organization names) must be kept in their original language and not translated.

<Output>
---Role---
You are a Knowledge Graph Specialist responsible for extracting entities and relationships from the input text.

---Instructions---
1.  **Entity Extraction & Output:**
    *   **Identification:** Identify clearly defined and meaningful entities in the input text.
    *   **Entity Details:** For each identified entity, extract the following information:
        *   `entity_name`: The name of the entity. If the entity name is case-insensitive, capitalize the first letter of each significant word (title case). Ensure **consistent naming** across the entire extraction process.
        *   `entity_type`: Categorize the entity using one of the following types: `Person,Creature,Organization,Location,Event,Concept,Method,Content,Data,Artifact,NaturalObject`. If none of the provided entity types apply, do not add new entity type and classify it as `Other`.
        *   `entity_description`: Provide a concise yet comprehensive description of the entity's attributes and activities, based *solely* on the information present in the input text.
    *   **Output Format - Entities:** Output a total of 4 fields for each entity, delimited by `<|#|>`, on a single line. The first field *must* be the literal string `entity`.
        *   Format: `entity<|#|>entity_name<|#|>entity_type<|#|>entity_description`

2.  **Relationship Extraction & Output:**
    *   **Identification:** Identify direct, clearly stated, and meaningful relationships between previously extracted entities.
    *   **N-ary Relationship Decomposition:** If a single statement describes a relationship involving more than two entities (an N-ary relationship), decompose it into multiple binary (two-entity) relationship pairs for separate description.
        *   **Example:** For "Alice, Bob, and Carol collaborated on Project X," extract binary relationships such as "Alice collaborated with Project X," "Bob collaborated with Project X," and "Carol collaborated with Project X," or "Alice collaborated with Bob," based on the most reasonable binary interpretations.
    *   **Relationship Details:** For each binary relationship, extract the following fields:
        *   `source_entity`: The name of the source entity. Ensure **consistent naming** with entity extraction. Capitalize the first letter of each significant word (title case) if the name is case-insensitive.
        *   `target_entity`: The name of the target entity. Ensure **consistent naming** with entity extraction. Capitalize the first letter of each significant word (title case) if the name is case-insensitive.
        *   `relationship_keywords`: One or more high-level keywords summarizing the overarching nature, concepts, or themes of the relationship. Multiple keywords within this field must be separated by a comma `,`. **DO NOT use `<|#|>` for separating multiple keywords within this field.**
        *   `relationship_description`: A concise explanation of the nature of the relationship between the source and target entities, providing a clear rationale for their connection.
    *   **Output Format - Relationships:** Output a total of 5 fields for each relationship, delimited by `<|#|>`, on a single line. The first field *must* be the literal string `relation`.
        *   Format: `relation<|#|>source_entity<|#|>target_entity<|#|>relationship_keywords<|#|>relationship_description`

3.  **Delimiter Usage Protocol:**
    *   The `<|#|>` is a complete, atomic marker and **must not be filled with content**. It serves strictly as a field separator.
    *   **Incorrect Example:** `entity<|#|>Tokyo<|location|>Tokyo is the capital of Japan.`
    *   **Correct Example:** `entity<|#|>Tokyo<|#|>location<|#|>Tokyo is the capital of Japan.`

4.  **Relationship Direction & Duplication:**
    *   Treat all relationships as **undirected** unless explicitly stated otherwise. Swapping the source and target entities for an undirected relationship does not constitute a new relationship.
    *   Avoid outputting duplicate relationships.

5.  **Output Order & Prioritization:**
    *   Output all extracted entities first, followed by all extracted relationships.
    *   Within the list of relationships, prioritize and output those relationships that are **most significant** to the core meaning of the input text first.

6.  **Context & Objectivity:**
    *   Ensure all entity names and descriptions are written in the **third person**.
    *   Explicitly name the subject or object; **avoid using pronouns** such as `this article`, `this paper`, `our company`, `I`, `you`, and `he/she`.

7.  **Language & Proper Nouns:**
    *   The entire output (entity names, keywords, and descriptions) must be written in `English`.
    *   Proper nouns (e.g., personal names, place names, organization names) should be retained in their original language if a proper, widely accepted translation is not available or would cause ambiguity.

8.  **Completion Signal:** Output the literal string `<|COMPLETE|>` only after all entities and relationships, following all criteria, have been completely extracted and outputted.

---Examples---

子宫内膜异位症 (简称内异症)是指子宫内膜组织 (腺体和间质)出现在子宫体以外的部位,其与子宫腺肌病统称为子宫内膜异位性疾病[1]。内异症是育龄期妇女常见的慢性疾病,其主要临床症状为疼痛与不孕 明显影响患者的生活质量 及早诊断对该病的早期治疗具有重要临床意义。超声检查是内异症诊断、治疗及随访的主要影像学手段,但不同级别医院超声医师在评估时仍存在检查不规范、对内异症复杂性认识不足、漏误诊较多,以及对深部浸润型内异症病变检出率较低等诸多问题 虽然中华医学会妇产科学分会子宫内膜异位症协作组已发表 子宫内膜异位症的诊治指南》第三版[2],但该临床指南未涉及相关超声检查规范 为此 中华医学会超声医学分会妇产超声学组组织国内有关专家,针对内异症超声检查常见问题,经过反复讨论,参考相关文献及国外共识与指南,并结合国内临床实践,形成本共识,旨在促进超声医师更加全面且规范地进行内异症的超声检查与评估。

问题一、内异症主要有哪些类型?

主要有4种类型 卵巢型内异症 即卵巢子宫内膜异位囊肿,俗称巧克力囊肿,是内异症最常见的类型 腹膜型内异症 指盆腔腹膜的浅层内异症种植病灶 由于腹膜型内异症位置表浅且常较小 常规超声检查不易显示病灶,主要依靠腹腔镜诊断; 深部浸 润 型 内 异 症 ( ,),指病灶浸润深度 5 ,包括子宫骶韧带、子宫直肠陷凹 ( , )、阴道穹隆、阴道直肠隔、直肠或结肠壁的内异症病变,也可侵犯至膀胱壁和输尿管; 其他部位内异症,包括瘢痕内异症及其他少见的远处内异症。

问题二、哪些临床线索及检查方法有助于内异症的超声评估?

1 相关病史、症状、体征等临床资料

内异症临床症状主要包括痛经、慢性盆腔痛、性交痛、肛门坠痛、月经异常等,约40 50 的患者合并不孕[1]。出现以下症状时需考虑相应部位的内异症: 周期性便秘或便频、便血、经期排便疼痛等提示可能有肠道 ; 经期尿频、尿急、尿痛甚至血尿提示可能为膀胱 瘢痕部位触及结节 有经期局部疼痛或结节增大时高度提示瘢痕内异症;规律性经期咯血提示肺部内异症; 肾积水时要考虑输尿管 的可能性

妇科双合诊触及活动度欠佳的盆腔囊性包块 阴道后穹隆、 、子宫骶韧带等部位有触痛性结节,子宫后倾固定等体征均提示内异症的可能。

2.选择合适的探头及扫查途径

内异症超声检查应尽量采用经阴道超声检查途径,尤其是疑诊 病灶及小的卵巢子宫内膜异位囊肿时[3-4]。对不能行经阴道超声检查的患者,可采用经直肠超声检查途径 检查前准备与经阴道超声一致,或可做简单的肠道准备以助获取更为清晰的图像。采用双平面探头及直肠360腔内高频探头等均有助于发现肠壁 病灶。

3 辅助扫查技巧

超声检查过程中,可采用一些辅助扫查手段获得更多诊断信息

直肠隔

阴道直肠隔指阴道后壁与直肠前壁之间的区域,范围从宫颈后唇下缘水平至阴道口 如 病灶位于宫颈后唇下缘水平以下 (腹膜返折线以下)时,可提示阴道直肠隔 [9] 超声表现 病灶位置较低呈结节状、长条形或不规则低回声区,偶可见部分无回声区[21]。该区域 可向前浸润累及阴道后壁,向后浸润累及直肠前壁 (图11)。

6 肠道

累及直肠和 (或)乙状结肠也较为常见,以直肠受累更常见[22-23]。病灶常呈多灶性,即同一节段肠壁见多个病灶或数个肠段均受累。超声表现: 在肠管纵切面可见病灶呈长条形、带状或结节状低回声,两端呈 “蝌蚪尾状”,边缘可不规则、呈特征性“印第安头饰”表现,即病灶边缘向肠腔方向延伸出多个 “羽毛状”低回声 (图12); 部分病灶边缘呈规则结节状 (图13); 肠管横切面可见病灶位于直肠或乙状结肠前壁,多呈楔形 (图14); 于病灶内可见散在血流信号 (图15); 肠道 病灶位于肠壁肌层及浆膜层,极少累及黏膜层。

因 病灶常累及盆腔多个部位,且常与卵巢型内异症并存,应注意对 好发的宫颈后区域(子宫骶韧带、 )、阴道直肠隔、直肠、乙状结肠等部位进行观察。发现可疑 病灶时,需对病灶部位及数目等进行详细描述,并注意评估盆腔粘连情况及有无肾积水等。

本共识建议:密切结合临床相关资料对深部浸润型内异症进行全面、规范的超声评估,并建议对疑诊病例常规评估泌尿系统情况。

问题六、瘢痕内异症主要有哪些? 其声像图特征 ?

瘢痕内异症主要指剖宫产术后腹壁切口瘢痕、会阴侧切口或撕裂处瘢痕内异症,前者病灶位于腹壁皮下脂肪层和 (或)腹壁肌层,后者病灶位于会阴部皮下脂肪和 (或)肌层内。

瘢痕内异症的超声特征: 病灶呈实性低回声,形态不规则 (图16),且大多内部回声不均匀 (图17),有时见大小不等囊性区域; 显示病灶内散在血流信号 (图18)。

会阴部瘢痕内异症病灶内有时可见较丰富的血流信号,与会阴部血供丰富有关。结合与月经周期相关的瘢痕部位疼痛等临床症状、体征,可做出相应诊断。

本共识建议:应重视与月经周期相关的瘢痕处疼痛主诉,使用高频探头对疼痛部位进行超声扫查,以发现瘢痕内异症病灶。

问题七、内异症的鉴别诊断要点有哪些?

卵巢子宫内膜异位囊肿需与卵巢出血性囊肿、畸胎瘤、卵巢上皮性肿瘤、盆腔脓肿等相鉴别;膀胱和直肠部位 需与膀胱癌和直肠癌相鉴别。

1 卵巢出血性囊肿内常见网状、条索状或粗细 不均的点状回声,超声随诊囊肿大小与回声的变化有 助于明确诊断

2 卵巢子宫内膜异位囊肿内较黏稠陈旧积血的回声较高,与 “磨玻璃样”回声成分间可表现为分层征,需与畸胎瘤的脂液分层征相鉴别。前者高回声部分位于下层、低回声部分位于上层,而后者高回声的脂质位于上层;此外,前者分层界面有时可随体位改变,且不一定呈直线。

3 卵巢子宫内膜异位囊肿内血凝块与卵巢上皮性肿瘤的乳头及实性病变鉴别要点: 检查块

问题四、卵巢子宫内膜异位囊肿有哪些声像图特征 ?

1 卵巢子宫内膜异位囊肿的典型超声特征[11-13]包括: 囊内容物呈 “磨玻璃样”回声 (也可描述为均匀密集的点状回声) (图1),囊壁较厚或厚薄不均,部分囊肿内可见中等或中高回声; 彩色多普勒血流显像 ( , )示囊肿内部无血流信号 仅在囊壁或分隔上见条状血流多数病灶呈单房,少数呈多房 (图2),或多发囊肿 (图3)。

4 妊娠期的卵巢子宫内膜异位囊肿约40 囊壁发生蜕膜化[15-16]。超声表现为囊壁明显增厚,可见多发乳头状突起,乳头形态较规则 (图6); 显示增厚的囊壁及乳头内均可见丰富血流信号 图7

5 内异症恶变的超声征象: 卵巢子宫内膜异位囊肿明显增大 (最大径 10 ); 囊内回声发生变化,出现实性回声结构,且 显示较丰富血流信号; 绝经后囊肿不缩小反而增大。以上征象均应警惕内异症恶变的可能性。

问题五、不同部位 有哪些声像图特征?1 膀胱

检查时膀胱适度充盈。超声表现: 膀胱壁内低回声病灶,呈结节状或不规则形,多位于膀胱后壁中线处,有时向膀胱腔内突出[17]; 病灶位于膀胱壁的肌层内,部分病例浆膜层受累,但极少累及黏膜层(图8); 显示病灶内散在血流信号; 子宫-膀胱陷凹常因粘连而封闭,阴道探头轻推之,子宫前壁与膀胱间的相对运动消失。

2 输尿管

绝大多数输尿管 为子宫骶韧带等后盆腔病灶累及所致,病灶压迫或直接累及输尿管造成梗阻,表现为患侧肾积水,严重者可导致重度肾积水 肾功能受损[18-19]

超声检查可见肾盂、输尿管积水的间接征象;有时于宫旁输尿管周围可见形态不规则的低回声病灶但若无输尿管扩张或肾积水,因输尿管 病灶较隐匿,常常被漏诊。

3 子宫骶韧带

子宫骶韧带是 最常累及部位之一,且常为双侧受累。正常情况下超声较难显示子宫骶韧带,有病灶时,超声表现为宫颈下段两侧边界不清的低回声结节 (图9),或条索状低回声区,横切面上病灶位于宫颈后外侧[20]

4 子宫直肠陷凹

位于子宫颈后方、阴道直肠隔之上,也是最常累及部位之一。超声表现: 病灶位于宫颈和 (或)宫体后方,边界不清 (图10); 子宫滑动征阴性; 病灶向下浸润累及阴道后穹隆、阴道直肠隔;向后浸润累及直肠前壁;向前浸润累及子宫浆膜层。

5 阴道直肠隔

阴道直肠隔指阴道后壁与直肠前壁之间的区域,范围从宫颈后唇下缘水平至阴道口 如 病灶位于宫颈后唇下缘水平以下 (腹膜返折线以下)时,可提示阴道直肠隔 [9] 超声表现 病灶位置较低呈结节状、长条形或不规则

经直肠超声检查途径 检查前准备与经阴道超声一致,或可做简单的肠道准备以助获取更为清晰的图像。采用双平面探头及直肠360腔内高频探头等均有助于发现肠壁 病灶。

3 辅助扫查技巧

超声检查过程中,可采用一些辅助扫查手段获得更多诊断信息: 触痛阳性引导法:指腔内探头移动过程中、或对临床触痛阳性部位轻压时注意患者的疼痛反应,于疼痛阳性区域仔细寻找病灶的检查方法[5],有助发现 病灶; 阴道或直肠生理盐水或凝胶超声成像法:经阴道超声检查时向阴道或直肠内注入生理盐水或凝胶,有助于观察阴道壁、阴道前后穹隆或直肠壁 病灶[6]; 检查前肠道准备:检查前进行适当肠道准备也有助于检出直肠、乙状结肠等部位的 病灶[7]

4 子宫滑动征检查法

子宫滑动征是检查 是否封闭的超声检查方法[8],易于操作与掌握,不需要复杂的检查技巧。具体操作方法如下: 子宫前位时,先用腔内探头轻推宫颈,观察宫颈与直肠前壁间有无相对运动,有相对运动则提示宫颈部位子宫滑动征阳性;然后用左手置于被检查者下腹部固定子宫体,再次轻推探头,观察直肠前壁与子宫后壁间有无相对运动 有相对运动则提示宫体部位子宫滑动征阳性 两部位滑动征均阳性可判断 未封闭,两部位或其中一部位滑动征阴性,判断 封闭; 子宫后位时,用腔内探头轻推子宫,观察子宫后壁及子宫底区域与直肠前壁间有无相对运动,有相对运动则该部位滑动征阳性,判断未封闭 无相对运动则该部位滑动征阴性 判断 封闭。

5 评估盆腔粘连情况

内异症常伴有盆腔粘连,内异症严重程度评分包括对卵巢等盆腔脏器粘连情况及 封闭情况的评估[2],因此,超声评估有无盆腔粘连有助于内异症诊断及其严重程度的判定[9]。

盆腔粘连的超声评估包括: 运用超声探头轻推等方法,观察卵巢与子宫、病灶与盆腔脏器间的相对运动; 应用子宫滑动征判断 有无封闭; 观察盆腔粘连的间接征象 如盆腔局限性积液 腹膜包涵囊肿、输卵管积水等,也有助于盆腔粘连的评估[10]

本共识推荐:对可疑内异症患者均应详细询问病史,了解相关临床症状及体征。检查时采用经腔内高频探头扫查途径,应用触痛阳性引导法、观察子宫滑动征、评估盆腔粘连情况等,可以提高超声对内异症病灶的诊断效能。

问题三、内异症的系统超声评估包括哪些步骤?

第一步:自阴道正中矢状切面开始,进行规范的子宫、附件常规超声检查,包括对阴道、阴道直肠隔、子宫、卵巢及输卵管的观察,并进行相应的测量。注意观察有无卵巢子宫内膜异位囊肿等。

第二步:寻找前、后盆腔可疑 病灶。应用探头触痛阳性引导法等仔细寻找 病灶,尤其需注意宫颈附近、 、直肠前壁、直肠-乙状结肠交界处、阴道直肠隔、子宫膀胱陷凹等区域有无异常回声。

第三步:观察卵巢活动度及子宫滑动征,注意盆腔有无局限性积液等,以评估盆腔脏器粘连及是否封闭 此外 可疑 时还应注意有无肾积水

本共识推荐:对可疑子宫内膜异位症患者,应进行包括以上3个步骤的系统超声评估。

Do not include any introductory or concluding remarks, explanations, or additional text before or after the list.\n3.  **Completion Signal:** Output `<|COMPLETE|>` as the final line after all relevant entities and relationships have been extracted and presented.\n4.  **Output Language:** Ensure the output language is English. Processed---\n<Entity_types>\n[Person,Creature,Organization,Location,Event,Concept,Method,Content,Data,Artifact,NaturalObject]\n\n<Input Text>\n```\n经直肠超声检查途径 检查前准备与经阴道超声一致,或可做简单的肠道准备以助获取更为清晰的图像。采用双平面探头及直肠360腔内高频探头等均有助于发现肠壁 病灶。\n\n3 辅助扫查技巧\n\n超声检查过程中,可采用一些辅助扫查手段获得更多诊断信息: 触痛阳性引导法:指腔内探头移动过程中、或对临床触痛阳性部位轻压时注意患者的疼痛反应,于疼痛阳性区域仔细寻找病灶的检查方法[5],有助发现 病灶; 阴道或直肠生理盐水或凝胶超声成像法:经阴道超声检查时向阴道或直肠内注入生理盐水或凝胶,有助于观察阴道壁、阴道前后穹隆或直肠壁 病灶[6]; 检查前肠道准备:检查前进行适当肠道准备也有助于检出直肠、乙状结肠等部位的 病灶[7]\n\n4 子宫滑动征检查法\n\n子宫滑动征是检查 是否封闭的超声检查方法[8],易于操作与掌握,不需要复杂的检查技巧。具体操作方法如下: 子宫前位时,先用腔内探头轻推宫颈,观察宫颈与直肠前壁间有无相对运动,有相对运动则提示宫颈部位子宫滑动征阳性;然后用左手置于被检查者下腹部固定子宫体,再次轻推探头,观察直肠前壁与子宫后壁间有无相对运动 有相对运动则提示宫体部位子宫滑动征阳性 两部位滑动征均阳性可判断 未封闭,两部位或其中一部位滑动征阴性,判断 封闭; 子宫后位时,用腔内探头轻推子宫,观察子宫后壁及子宫底区域与直肠前壁间有无相对运动,有相对运动则该部位滑动征阳性,判断未封闭 无相对运动则该部位滑动征阴性 判断 封闭。\n\n5 评估盆腔粘连情况\n\n内异症常伴有盆腔粘连,内异症严重程度评分包括对卵巢等盆腔脏器粘连情况及 封闭情况的评估[2],因此,超声评估有无盆腔粘连有助于内异症诊断及其严重程度的判定[9]。\n\n盆腔粘连的超声评估包括: ---Role---
You are a Knowledge Graph Specialist responsible for extracting entities and relationships from the input text.

---Instructions---
1.  **Entity Extraction & Output:**
    *   **Identification:** Identify clearly defined and meaningful entities in the input text.
    *   **Entity Details:** For each identified entity, extract the following information:
        *   `entity_name`: The name of the entity. If the entity name is case-insensitive, capitalize the first letter of each significant word (title case). Ensure **consistent naming** across the entire extraction process.
        *   `entity_type`: Categorize the entity using one of the following types: `Person,Creature,Organization,Location,Event,Concept,Method,Content,Data,Artifact,NaturalObject`. If none of the provided entity types apply, do not add new entity type and classify it as `Other`.
        *   `entity_description`: Provide a concise yet comprehensive description of the entity's attributes and activities, based *solely* on the information present in the input text.
    *   **Output Format - Entities:** Output a total of 4 fields for each entity, delimited by `<|#|>`, on a single line. The first field *must* be the literal string `entity`.
        *   Format: `entity<|#|>entity_name<|#|>entity_type<|#|>entity_description`

2.  **Relationship Extraction & Output:**
    *   **Identification:** Identify direct, clearly stated, and meaningful relationships between previously extracted entities.
    *   **N-ary Relationship Decomposition:** If a single statement describes a relationship involving more than two entities (an N-ary relationship), decompose it into multiple binary (two-entity) relationship pairs for separate description.
        *   **Example:** For "Alice, Bob, and Carol collaborated on Project X," extract binary relationships such as "Alice collaborated with Project X," "Bob collaborated with Project X," and "Carol collaborated with Project X," or "Alice collaborated with Bob," based on the most reasonable binary interpretations.
    *   **Relationship Details:** For each binary relationship, extract the following fields:
        *   `source_entity`: The name of the source entity. Ensure **consistent naming** with entity extraction. Capitalize the first letter of each significant word (title case) if the name is case-insensitive.
        *   `target_entity`: The name of the target entity. Ensure **consistent naming** with entity extraction. Capitalize the first letter of each significant word (title case) if the name is case-insensitive.
        *   `relationship_keywords`: One or more high-level keywords summarizing the overarching nature, concepts, or themes of the relationship. Multiple keywords within this field must be separated by a comma `,`. **DO NOT use `<|#|>` for separating multiple keywords within this field.**
        *   `relationship_description`: A concise explanation of the nature of the relationship between the source and target entities, providing a clear rationale for their connection.
    *   **Output Format - Relationships:** Output a total of 5 fields for each relationship, delimited by `<|#|>`, on a single line. The first field *must* be the literal string `relation`.
        *   Format: `relation<|#|>source_entity<|#|>target_entity<|#|>relationship_keywords<|#|>relationship_description`

3.  **Delimiter Usage Protocol:**
    *   The `<|#|>` is a complete, atomic marker and **must not be filled with content**. It serves strictly as a field separator.
    *   **Incorrect Example:** `entity<|#|>Tokyo<|location|>Tokyo is the capital of Japan.`
    *   **Correct Example:** `entity<|#|>Tokyo<|#|>location<|#|>Tokyo is the capital of Japan.`

4.  **Relationship Direction & Duplication:**
    *   Treat all relationships as **undirected** unless explicitly stated otherwise. Swapping the source and target entities for an undirected relationship does not constitute a new relationship.
    *   Avoid outputting duplicate relationships.

5.  **Output Order & Prioritization:**
    *   Output all extracted entities first, followed by all extracted relationships.
    *   Within the list of relationships, prioritize and output those relationships that are **most significant** to the core meaning of the input text first.

6.  **Context & Objectivity:**
    *   Ensure all entity names and descriptions are written in the **third person**.
    *   Explicitly name the subject or object; **avoid using pronouns** such as `this article`, `this paper`, `our company`, `I`, `you`, and `he/she`.

7.  **Language & Proper Nouns:**
    *   The entire output (entity names, keywords, and descriptions) must be written in `English`.
    *   Proper nouns (e.g., personal names, place names, organization names) should be retained in their original language if a proper, widely accepted translation is not available or would cause ambiguity.

8.  **Completion Signal:** Output the literal string `<|COMPLETE|>` only after all entities and relationships, following all criteria, have been completely extracted and outputted.

---Task---
Based on the last extraction task, identify and extract any **missed or incorrectly formatted** entities and relationships from the input text.

---Instructions---
1.  **Strict Adherence to System Format:** Strictly adhere to all format requirements for entity and relationship lists, including output order, field delimiters, and proper noun handling, as specified in the system instructions.
2.  **Focus on Corrections/Additions:**
    *   **Do NOT** re-output entities and relationships that were **correctly and fully** extracted in the last task.
    *   If an entity or relationship was **missed** in the last task, extract and output it now according to the system format.
    *   If an entity or relationship was **truncated, had missing fields, or was otherwise incorrectly formatted** in the last task, re-output the *corrected and complete* version in the specified format.
3.  **Output Format - Entities:** Output a total of 4 fields for each entity, delimited by `<|#|>`, on a single line. The first field *must* be the literal string `entity`.
4.  **Output Format - Relationships:** Output a total of 5 fields for each relationship, delimited by `<|#|>`, on a single line. The first field *must* be the literal string `relation`.
5.  **Output Content Only:** Output *only* the extracted list of entities and relationships. Do not include any introductory or concluding remarks, explanations, or additional text before or after the list.
6.  **Completion Signal:** Output `<|COMPLETE|>` as the final line after all relevant missing or corrected entities and relationships have been extracted and presented.
7.  **Output Language:** Ensure the output language is English. Proper nouns (e.g., personal names, place names, organization names) must be kept in their original language and not translated.

<Output>
---Role---
You are a Knowledge Graph Specialist responsible for extracting entities and relationships from the input text.

---Instructions---
1.  **Entity Extraction & Output:**
    *   **Identification:** Identify clearly defined and meaningful entities in the input text.
    *   **Entity Details:** For each identified entity, extract the following information:
        *   `entity_name`: The name of the entity. If the entity name is case-insensitive, capitalize the first letter of each significant word (title case). Ensure **consistent naming** across the entire extraction process.
        *   `entity_type`: Categorize the entity using one of the following types: `Person,Creature,Organization,Location,Event,Concept,Method,Content,Data,Artifact,NaturalObject`. If none of the provided entity types apply, do not add new entity type and classify it as `Other`.
        *   `entity_description`: Provide a concise yet comprehensive description of the entity's attributes and activities, based *solely* on the information present in the input text.
    *   **Output Format - Entities:** Output a total of 4 fields for each entity, delimited by `<|#|>`, on a single line. The first field *must* be the literal string `entity`.
        *   Format: `entity<|#|>entity_name<|#|>entity_type<|#|>entity_description`

2.  **Relationship Extraction & Output:**
    *   **Identification:** Identify direct, clearly stated, and meaningful relationships between previously extracted entities.
    *   **N-ary Relationship Decomposition:** If a single statement describes a relationship involving more than two entities (an N-ary relationship), decompose it into multiple binary (two-entity) relationship pairs for separate description.
        *   **Example:** For "Alice, Bob, and Carol collaborated on Project X," extract binary relationships such as "Alice collaborated with Project X," "Bob collaborated with Project X," and "Carol collaborated with Project X," or "Alice collaborated with Bob," based on the most reasonable binary interpretations.
    *   **Relationship Details:** For each binary relationship, extract the following fields:
        *   `source_entity`: The name of the source entity. Ensure **consistent naming** with entity extraction. Capitalize the first letter of each significant word (title case) if the name is case-insensitive.
        *   `target_entity`: The name of the target entity. Ensure **consistent naming** with entity extraction. Capitalize the first letter of each significant word (title case) if the name is case-insensitive.
        *   `relationship_keywords`: One or more high-level keywords summarizing the overarching nature, concepts, or themes of the relationship. Multiple keywords within this field must be separated by a comma `,`. **DO NOT use `<|#|>` for separating multiple keywords within this field.**
        *   `relationship_description`: A concise explanation of the nature of the relationship between the source and target entities, providing a clear rationale for their connection.
    *   **Output Format - Relationships:** Output a total of 5 fields for each relationship, delimited by `<|#|>`, on a single line. The first field *must* be the literal string `relation`.
        *   Format: `relation<|#|>source_entity<|#|>target_entity<|#|>relationship_keywords<|#|>relationship_description`

3.  **Delimiter Usage Protocol:**
    *   The `<|#|>` is a complete, atomic marker and **must not be filled with content**. It serves strictly as a field separator.
    *   **Incorrect Example:** `entity<|#|>Tokyo<|location|>Tokyo is the capital of Japan.`
    *   **Correct Example:** `entity<|#|>Tokyo<|#|>location<|#|>Tokyo is the capital of Japan.`

4.  **Relationship Direction & Duplication:**
    *   Treat all relationships as **undirected** unless explicitly stated otherwise. Swapping the source and target entities for an undirected relationship does not constitute a new relationship.
    *   Avoid outputting duplicate relationships.

5.  **Output Order & Prioritization:**
    *   Output all extracted entities first, followed by all extracted relationships.
    *   Within the list of relationships, prioritize and output those relationships that are **most significant** to the core meaning of the input text first.

6.  **Context & Objectivity:**
    *   Ensure all entity names and descriptions are written in the **third person**.
    *   Explicitly name the subject or object; **avoid using pronouns** such as `this article`, `this paper`, `our company`, `I`, `you`, and `he/she`.

7.  **Language & Proper Nouns:**
    *   The entire output (entity names, keywords, and descriptions) must be written in `English`.
    *   Proper nouns (e.g., personal names, place names, organization names) should be retained in their original language if a proper, widely accepted translation is not available or would cause ambiguity.

8.  **Completion Signal:** Output the literal string `<|COMPLETE|>` only after all entities and relationships, following all criteria, have been completely extracted and outputted.

---Examples---

---Role---
You are a Knowledge Graph Specialist responsible for extracting entities and relationships from the input text.

---Instructions---
1.  **Entity Extraction & Output:**
    *   **Identification:** Identify clearly defined and meaningful entities in the input text.
    *   **Entity Details:** For each identified entity, extract the following information:
        *   `entity_name`: The name of the entity. If the entity name is case-insensitive, capitalize the first letter of each significant word (title case). Ensure **consistent naming** across the entire extraction process.
        *   `entity_type`: Categorize the entity using one of the following types: `Person,Creature,Organization,Location,Event,Concept,Method,Content,Data,Artifact,NaturalObject`. If none of the provided entity types apply, do not add new entity type and classify it as `Other`.
        *   `entity_description`: Provide a concise yet comprehensive description of the entity's attributes and activities, based *solely* on the information present in the input text.
    *   **Output Format - Entities:** Output a total of 4 fields for each entity, delimited by `<|#|>`, on a single line. The first field *must* be the literal string `entity`.
        *   Format: `entity<|#|>entity_name<|#|>entity_type<|#|>entity_description`

2.  **Relationship Extraction & Output:**
    *   **Identification:** Identify direct, clearly stated, and meaningful relationships between previously extracted entities.
    *   **N-ary Relationship Decomposition:** If a single statement describes a relationship involving more than two entities (an N-ary relationship), decompose it into multiple binary (two-entity) relationship pairs for separate description.
        *   **Example:** For "Alice, Bob, and Carol collaborated on Project X," extract binary relationships such as "Alice collaborated with Project X," "Bob collaborated with Project X," and "Carol collaborated with Project X," or "Alice collaborated with Bob," based on the most reasonable binary interpretations.
    *   **Relationship Details:** For each binary relationship, extract the following fields:
        *   `source_entity`: The name of the source entity. Ensure **consistent naming** with entity extraction. Capitalize the first letter of each significant word (title case) if the name is case-insensitive.
        *   `target_entity`: The name of the target entity. Ensure **consistent naming** with entity extraction. Capitalize the first letter of each significant word (title case) if the name is case-insensitive.
        *   `relationship_keywords`: One or more high-level keywords summarizing the overarching nature, concepts, or themes of the relationship. Multiple keywords within this field must be separated by a comma `,`. **DO NOT use `<|#|>` for separating multiple keywords within this field.**
        *   `relationship_description`: A concise explanation of the nature of the relationship between the source and target entities, providing a clear rationale for their connection.
    *   **Output Format - Relationships:** Output a total of 5 fields for each relationship, delimited by `<|#|>`, on a single line. The first field *must* be the literal string `relation`.
        *   Format: `relation<|#|>source_entity<|#|>target_entity<|#|>relationship_keywords<|#|>relationship_description`

3.  **Delimiter Usage Protocol:**
    *   The `<|#|>` is a complete, atomic marker and **must not be filled with content**. It serves strictly as a field separator.
    *   **Incorrect Example:** `entity<|#|>Tokyo<|location|>Tokyo is the capital of Japan.`
    *   **Correct Example:** `entity<|#|>Tokyo<|#|>location<|#|>Tokyo is the capital of Japan.`

4.  **Relationship Direction & Duplication:**
    *   Treat all relationships as **undirected** unless explicitly stated otherwise. Swapping the source and target entities for an undirected relationship does not constitute a new relationship.
    *   Avoid outputting duplicate relationships.

5.  **Output Order & Prioritization:**
    *   Output all extracted entities first, followed by all extracted relationships.
    *   Within the list of relationships, prioritize and output those relationships that are **most significant** to the core meaning of the input text first.

6.  **Context & Objectivity:**
    *   Ensure all entity names and descriptions are written in the **third person**.
    *   Explicitly name the subject or object; **avoid using pronouns** such as `this article`, `this paper`, `our company`, `I`, `you`, and `he/she`.

7.  **Language & Proper Nouns:**
    *   The entire output (entity names, keywords, and descriptions) must be written in `English`.
    *   Proper nouns (e.g., personal names, place names, organization names) should be retained in their original language if a proper, widely accepted translation is not available or would cause ambiguity.

8.  **Completion Signal:** Output the literal string `<|COMPLETE|>` only after all entities and relationships, following all criteria, have been completely extracted and outputted.

3 卵巢子宫内膜异位囊肿内血凝块与卵巢上皮性肿瘤的乳头及实性病变鉴别要点: 检查块状有回声区内有无血流是关键,卵巢肿瘤的乳头状结构及实性病变内一般可见血流信号,尤其是交界性或恶性肿瘤 若 鉴别困难时 超声造影有助明确诊断; 应用探头轻推或改变体位,观察囊内回声的移动性也有助于鉴别。

4 多房性卵巢子宫内膜异位囊肿与卵巢其他的多房性囊性肿瘤的鉴别要点:前者囊内容物呈 “磨玻璃样”回声、囊壁相对较厚,后者多无此表现,有助鉴别。但有时卵巢子宫内膜异位囊肿与卵巢黏液性上皮肿瘤的声像图表现有较多重叠,鉴别仍较困难。

5 妊娠期卵巢子宫内膜异位囊肿蜕膜化需与卵巢肿瘤相鉴别,病史及超声随访观察对鉴别诊断有较大帮助。

6 卵巢子宫内膜异位囊肿与盆腔脓肿的鉴别要点: 盆腔脓肿临床常有发热、下腹疼痛及明显压痛等急性感染症状;而前者在未合并感染的情况下一般不出现上述临床表现; 声像图上盆腔脓肿囊壁厚薄不均,各囊相通,呈管道状结构; 卵巢子宫内膜异位囊肿合并感染时与盆腔脓肿鉴别较为困难。有卵巢子宫内膜异位囊肿病史、抗感染治疗后声像图的变化等有助于鉴别。

7 膀胱和直肠部位 需与膀胱癌和直肠癌相鉴别: 病灶超声表现为浆膜层及肌层受累,很少累及黏膜层,且黏膜层光滑;而膀胱癌、直肠癌则病灶位于黏膜层,并呈乳头状或菜花状向管腔内生长,肿瘤晚期才会侵及管壁全层。

问题八、超声检查评估 准确性如何? 存在哪些局限性?

具有病变广泛、形态多样、侵袭性和复发性等特点 病变部位隐匿 不易发现 超声检查较易漏诊。国外文献荟萃分析显示[21,24-25],经阴道超声检查对 病灶检出的总敏感度785 853 ,特异度461 925 ,诊断准确率757 97 。各部位 超声检查的敏感度与特异度分别为:直肠、乙状结肠333 981 与86 100 子宫骶韧带10 889 与75 996 阴道直肠隔3662 与95 99 ,膀胱20 100 与964100 。文献报道的超声评估 的准确性差异较大。目前国内尚无相关大样本统计数据报道,规范化超声评估 的临床研究数据有待积累。

超声评估 存在以下局限性 当 病灶累及肠道等部位时,超声检查较易发现病灶;但当病灶位于子宫骶韧带、阴道直肠隔等部位时,超声检查对病灶的检出率均不高 即使相对易观察区域,超声检查也较难发现较小的 病灶; 超声检查尚难以对盆腔所有 病灶进行全面、准确的定位评估。

结语

超声是内异症首选的无创影像学检查方法。不同部位的内异症有其相应的特征性声像图表现,有些病灶超声表现复杂、多变,且常见多个部位内异症共存的情况 对一些 病灶 超声在发现及准确定位方面仍存在一定局限性,对少数表现不典型卵巢子宫内膜异位囊肿的鉴别诊断也仍较困难 对可疑内异症患者进行超声检查时,应密切结合病史、症状、体征等临床资料,充分掌握内异症相关检查方法、途径、技巧及声像图特征,进行全面、规范的超声评估,为内异
```

---Instructions---
1.  **Entity Extraction & Output:**

        *   Format: `entity<|#|>entity_name<|#|>entity_type<|#|>entity_description`

2.  **Relationship Extraction & Output:**
    *   **Identification:** Identify direct, clearly stated, and meaningful relationships between previously extracted entities.
    *   **N-ary Relationship Decomposition:** If a single statement describes a relationship involving more than two entities (an N-ary relationship), decompose it into multiple binary (two-entity) relationship pairs for separate description.
        *   **Example:** For "Alice, Bob, and Carol collaborated on Project X," extract binary relationships such as "Alice collaborated with Project X," "Bob collaborated with Project X," and "Carol collaborated with Project X," or "Alice collaborated with Bob," based on the most reasonable binary interpretations.
    *   **Relationship Details:** For each binary relationship, extract the following fields:
        *   `source_entity`: The name of the source entity. Ensure **consistent naming** with entity extraction. Capitalize the first letter of each significant word (title case) if the name is case-insensitive.
        *   `target_entity`: The name of the target entity. Ensure **consistent naming** with entity extraction. Capitalize the first letter of each significant word (title case) if the name is case-insensitive.
        *   `relationship_keywords`: One or more high-level keywords summarizing the overarching nature, concepts, or themes of the relationship. Multiple keywords within this field must be separated by a comma `,`. **DO NOT use `<|#|>` for separating multiple keywords within this field.**
        *   `relationship_description`: A concise explanation of the nature of the relationship between the source and target entities, providing a clear rationale for their connection.
    *   **Output Format - Relationships:** Output a total of 5 fields for each relationship, delimited by `<|#|>`, on a single line. The first field *must* be the literal string `relation`.
        *   Format: `relation<|#|>source_entity<|#|>target_entity<|#|>relationship_keywords<|#|>relationship_description`

3.  **Delimiter Usage Protocol:**

    *   **Incorrect Example:** `entity<|#|>Tokyo<|location|>Tokyo is the capital of Japan.`
    *   **Correct Example:** `entity<|#|>Tokyo<|#|>location<|#|>Tokyo is the capital of Japan.`

4.  **Relationship Direction & Duplication:**
    *   Treat all relationships as **undirected** unless explicitly stated otherwise. Swapping the source and target entities for an undirected relationship does not constitute a new relationship.
    *   Avoid outputting duplicate relationships.

5.  **Output Order & Prioritization:**

    *   Within the list of relationships, prioritize and output those relationships that are **most significant** to the core meaning of the input text first.

6.  **Context & Objectivity:**

Text>\n```\n部分位于下层、低回声部分位于上层,而后者高回声的脂质位于上层;此外,前者分层界面有时可随体位改变,且不一定呈直线。\n\n3 卵巢子宫内膜异位囊肿内血凝块与卵巢上皮性肿瘤的乳头及实性病变鉴别要点: 检查块状有回声区内有无血流是关键,卵巢肿瘤的乳头状结构及实性病变内一般可见血流信号,尤其是交界性或恶性肿瘤 若 鉴别困难时 超声造影有助明确诊断; 应用探头轻推或改变体位,观察囊内回声的移动性也有助于鉴别。\n\n4 多房性卵巢子宫内膜异位囊肿与卵巢其他的多房性囊性肿瘤的鉴别要点:前者囊内容物呈 “磨玻璃样”回声、囊壁相对较厚,后者多无此表现,有助鉴别。但有时卵巢子宫内膜异位囊肿与卵巢黏液性上皮肿瘤的声像图表现有较多重叠,鉴别仍较困难。\n\n5 妊娠期卵巢子宫内膜异位囊肿蜕膜化需与卵巢肿瘤相鉴别,病史及超声随访观察对鉴别诊断有较大帮助。\n\n6 卵巢子宫内膜异位囊肿与盆腔脓肿的鉴别要点: 盆腔脓肿临床常有发热、下腹疼痛及明显压痛等急性感染症状;而前者在未合并感染的情况下一般不出现上述临床表现; 声像图上盆腔脓肿囊壁厚薄不均,各囊相通,呈管道状结构; 卵巢子宫内膜异位囊肿合并感染时与盆腔脓肿鉴别较为困难。有卵巢子宫内膜异位囊肿病史、抗感染治疗后声像图的变化等有助于鉴别。\n\n7 膀胱和直肠部位 需与膀胱癌和直肠癌相鉴别: 病灶超声表现为浆膜层及肌层受累,很少累及黏膜层,且黏膜层光滑;而膀胱癌、直肠癌则病灶位于黏膜层,并呈乳头状或菜花状向管腔内生长,肿瘤晚期才会侵及管壁全层。\n\n问题八、超声检查评估 准确性如何? 存在哪些局限性?\n\n具有病变广泛、形态多样、侵袭性和复发性等特点 病变部位隐匿 不易发现 超声检查较易漏诊。国外文献荟萃分析显示[21,24-25],经阴道超声检查对 病灶检出的总敏感度785 853 ,特异度461 925 ,诊断准确率757 97 。各部位 超声检查的敏感度与特异度分别为:直肠、乙状结肠333 981 与86 100 子宫骶韧带10 889 与75 996 ---Role---
You are a Knowledge Graph Specialist responsible for extracting entities and relationships from the input text.

---Instructions---
1.  **Entity Extraction & Output:**
    *   **Identification:** Identify clearly defined and meaningful entities in the input text.
    *   **Entity Details:** For each identified entity, extract the following information:
        *   `entity_name`: The name of the entity. If the entity name is case-insensitive, capitalize the first letter of each significant word (title case). Ensure **consistent naming** across the entire extraction process.
        *   `entity_type`: Categorize the entity using one of the following types: `Person,Creature,Organization,Location,Event,Concept,Method,Content,Data,Artifact,NaturalObject`. If none of the provided entity types apply, do not add new entity type and classify it as `Other`.
        *   `entity_description`: Provide a concise yet comprehensive description of the entity's attributes and activities, based *solely* on the information present in the input text.
    *   **Output Format - Entities:** Output a total of 4 fields for each entity, delimited by `<|#|>`, on a single line. The first field *must* be the literal string `entity`.
        *   Format: `entity<|#|>entity_name<|#|>entity_type<|#|>entity_description`

2.  **Relationship Extraction & Output:**
    *   **Identification:** Identify direct, clearly stated, and meaningful relationships between previously extracted entities.
    *   **N-ary Relationship Decomposition:** If a single statement describes a relationship involving more than two entities (an N-ary relationship), decompose it into multiple binary (two-entity) relationship pairs for separate description.
        *   **Example:** For "Alice, Bob, and Carol collaborated on Project X," extract binary relationships such as "Alice collaborated with Project X," "Bob collaborated with Project X," and "Carol collaborated with Project X," or "Alice collaborated with Bob," based on the most reasonable binary interpretations.
    *   **Relationship Details:** For each binary relationship, extract the following fields:
        *   `source_entity`: The name of the source entity. Ensure **consistent naming** with entity extraction. Capitalize the first letter of each significant word (title case) if the name is case-insensitive.
        *   `target_entity`: The name of the target entity. Ensure **consistent naming** with entity extraction. Capitalize the first letter of each significant word (title case) if the name is case-insensitive.
        *   `relationship_keywords`: One or more high-level keywords summarizing the overarching nature, concepts, or themes of the relationship. Multiple keywords within this field must be separated by a comma `,`. **DO NOT use `<|#|>` for separating multiple keywords within this field.**
        *   `relationship_description`: A concise explanation of the nature of the relationship between the source and target entities, providing a clear rationale for their connection.
    *   **Output Format - Relationships:** Output a total of 5 fields for each relationship, delimited by `<|#|>`, on a single line. The first field *must* be the literal string `relation`.
        *   Format: `relation<|#|>source_entity<|#|>target_entity<|#|>relationship_keywords<|#|>relationship_description`

3.  **Delimiter Usage Protocol:**
    *   The `<|#|>` is a complete, atomic marker and **must not be filled with content**. It serves strictly as a field separator.
    *   **Incorrect Example:** `entity<|#|>Tokyo<|location|>Tokyo is the capital of Japan.`
    *   **Correct Example:** `entity<|#|>Tokyo<|#|>location<|#|>Tokyo is the capital of Japan.`

4.  **Relationship Direction & Duplication:**
    *   Treat all relationships as **undirected** unless explicitly stated otherwise. Swapping the source and target entities for an undirected relationship does not constitute a new relationship.
    *   Avoid outputting duplicate relationships.

5.  **Output Order & Prioritization:**
    *   Output all extracted entities first, followed by all extracted relationships.
    *   Within the list of relationships, prioritize and output those relationships that are **most significant** to the core meaning of the input text first.

6.  **Context & Objectivity:**
    *   Ensure all entity names and descriptions are written in the **third person**.
    *   Explicitly name the subject or object; **avoid using pronouns** such as `this article`, `this paper`, `our company`, `I`, `you`, and `he/she`.

7.  **Language & Proper Nouns:**
    *   The entire output (entity names, keywords, and descriptions) must be written in `English`.
    *   Proper nouns (e.g., personal names, place names, organization names) should be retained in their original language if a proper, widely accepted translation is not available or would cause ambiguity.

8.  **Completion Signal:** Output the literal string `<|COMPLETE|>` only after all entities and relationships, following all criteria, have been completely extracted and outputted.

---Role---
You are a Knowledge Graph Specialist, proficient in data curation and synthesis.

---Task---
Your task is to synthesize a list of descriptions of a given entity or relation into a single, comprehensive, and cohesive summary.

---Instructions---
1. Input Format: The description list is provided in JSON format. Each JSON object (representing a single description) appears on a new line within the `Description List` section.
2. Output Format: The merged description will be returned as plain text, presented in multiple paragraphs, without any additional formatting or extraneous comments before or after the summary.
3. Comprehensiveness: The summary must integrate all key information from *every* provided description. Do not omit any important facts or details.
4. Context: Ensure the summary is written from an objective, third-person perspective; explicitly mention the name of the entity or relation for full clarity and context.
5. Context & Objectivity:
  - Write the summary from an objective, third-person perspective.
  - Explicitly mention the full name of the entity or relation at the beginning of the summary to ensure immediate clarity and context.
6. Conflict Handling:
  - In cases of conflicting or inconsistent descriptions, first determine if these conflicts arise from multiple, distinct entities or relationships that share the same name.
  - If distinct entities/relations are identified, summarize each one *separately* within the overall output.
  - If conflicts within a single entity/relation (e.g., historical discrepancies) exist, attempt to reconcile them or present both viewpoints with noted uncertainty.
7. Length Constraint:The summary's total length must not exceed 600 tokens, while still maintaining depth and completeness.
8. Language: The entire output must be written in English. Proper nouns (e.g., personal names, place names, organization names) may in their original language if proper translation is not available.
  - The entire output must be written in English.
  - Proper nouns (e.g., personal names, place names, organization names) should be retained in their original language if a proper, widely accepted translation is not available or would cause ambiguity.

---Input---
Entity Name: Regulatory Uncertainty

Description List:
